To enable the propeller RPM to be increased without accompanying increase in engine RPM.

questions.questionText: The mechanism to change the propeller blade angle of a small piston engine aeroplane in flight is operated:
The slipstream effect of a propeller is most prominent at:
options: low airspeeds with high power setting.
high airspeeds with high power setting.
low airspeeds with low power setting.
high airspeeds with low power setting.

questions.questionText: The blade angle of a constant-speed propeller.
options: decreases with increasing true air speed.
is independent of the true air speed.
increases with increasing true air speed.

questions.questionText: A pilot can actuate the feathering system by:
options: pulling the propeller control lever rearwards.
pushing the propeller control lever forwards.

questions.questionText: An asymmetric loading (p-factor) on the propeller exists:
options: only if the "constant speed propeller" mechanism is broken.
if the aeroplane has a large angle of attack.
if there is an unbalanced propeller.

questions.questionText: Concerning a constant speed propeller of a twin engine aeroplane controlled by a single-acting pitch control unit:
options: the spring force turns the propeller blades towards smaller pitch angle.
the oil pressure turns the propeller blades towards higher pitch angle.
the oil pressure turns the propeller blades towards smaller pitch angle.
the aerodynamic force turns the propeller blades towards higher pitch angle.

questions.questionText: When in flight, a piston engine is stopped and the propeller blade pitch angle is near 90°, the propeller is said to be:
When TAS increases, the blade angle of a constant speed propeller will (RPM and throttle levers are not moved):
options: first reduce and after a short time increase to its previous value.

questions.questionText: Fixed pitch propellers are usually designed for maximum efficiency at:
For take-off, the correct combination of propeller pitch and propeller lever position at brake release is:
A propeller blade is twisted in order to:
options: reduce the blade tangential velocity from root to tip.
maintain a constant angle of attack along the blade.
avoid the appearance of sonic phenomena.

questions.questionText: Excessive pressure in the cylinders of an engine equipped with a constant speed propeller, can be caused by the combination of:
options: low manifold pressure and high RPM.
high manifold pressure and low RPM.
high manifold pressure and high RPM.
low manifold pressure and low RPM.

questions.questionText: Spark plug fouling is more likely to happen if:
options: the engine runs at maximum continuous power for too long.
the aircraft descends for a prolonged time at idle power.
the aircraft cruises at low speed with the correct mixture.

questions.questionText: The conditions most likely to produce the highest engine power are:
options: cold and humid air at high pressure.
warm and dry air at high pressure.
cold and dry air at high pressure.
warm and humid air at low pressure.

questions.questionText: During climb with constant throttle and RPM lever setting (mixture being constant) the:
When changing power on engines equipped with constant-speed propeller, engine overload is avoided by.
options: adjusting fuel flow before the manifold pressure.
reducing the RPM before reducing the manifold pressure.
increasing the manifold pressure before increasing the RPM.
increasing the RPM before increasing the manifold pressure.

questions.questionText: A turbocharger in a reciprocating engine consists of a:
options: compressor and turbine mounted on a common shaft.
compressor driving a turbine via a reduction gear.
turbine driving a compressor via a reduction gear.
compressor and turbine on individual shafts.

questions.questionText: The maximum horsepower output which can be obtained from an engine when it is operated at specified RPM and manifold pressure conditions established as safe for continuous operation is termed:
One of the advantages of a turbocharger is:
options: the power available is less affected by altitude.
that there is no danger of detonation.
that there is no torsion at the crankshaft.

questions.questionText: The kind of compressor normally used as a supercharger is:
On a normally aspirated aero-engine fitted with a fixed pitch propeller.
options: manifold pressure decreases as the aircraft climbs at a fixed throttle setting.
the propeller angle of attack is constant at all indicated airspeeds.
in level flight, manifold pressure will remain constant when the RPM is increased by opening the throttle.
in a descent at a fixed throttle setting manifold pressure will always remain constant.

questions.questionText: The power output of a normally aspirated piston engine increases with increasing altitude at constant Manifold Absolute Pressure (MAP) and RPM because of the:
options: lower losses during the gas change.

questions.questionText: In a piston engine, turbocharger boost pressure may be monitored by:
options: both engine RPM readings and a manifold pressure gauge.
a cylinder head temperature gauge (CHT), a manifold pressure gauge, and engine RPM readings.
both a CHT gauge and manifold pressure gauge.

questions.questionText: With which instrument(s) do you monitor the power output of an aeroplane fitted with a fixed pitch propeller?
The air in a piston engine supercharger enters the centrifugal compressor at:
options: a tangent to the rotor and leaves via the stator.
the periphery and leaves via the eye of the impeller.
the eye of the impeller and leaves it almost at a tangent to the periphery.
the diffuser and is fed to the impeller at the optimum angle of attack.

questions.questionText: Prolonged running at low RPM may have an adverse effect on the efficiency of the:
The power output of a piston engine without turbocharging increases with increasing altitude in standard atmosphere at constant MAP and RPM because of the:
options: increase of the air density behind the throttle valve.

questions.questionText: If an engine fails to stop with the magneto switch in OFF position, the cause may be:
options: excessive carbon formation in cylinder head.

questions.questionText: On a normally aspirated engine, the manifold pressure gauge always indicates:
options: a greater value than atmospheric pressure when the engine is running.
a lower value than atmospheric pressure when the engine is running.
zero on the ground when the engine is stopped.
a value equal to the atmospheric pressure when the engine is at full power on the ground.

questions.questionText: The rating of electrical fuses is expressed in:
The true statement among the following in relation to the application of Ohm's law is:
options: current in a circuit is inversely proportional to the applied electromotive force.
the current in a circuit is directly proportional to the resistance of the circuit.
current in a circuit is directly proportional to the applied electromotive force.
power in the circuit is inversely proportional to the square of the current.

questions.questionText:
Refer to figure 021-07
The logic symbol 3 represents (assuming positive logic):
One indication of inadequate electrical bonding of aircraft components may be:
options: heavy corrosion on the fuselage skin mountings.

questions.questionText: Because of the input/output relationship of an OR gate, it is often referred to as the:
Bleeding air from the HP compressor of a turbofan, whilst maintaining thrust, increases:
options: HP compressor discharge pressure and N2.

questions.questionText: Derated or reduced thrust take-offs save engine life primarily due to:
options: reduced creep damage due to higher EGT levels during take-off.
the maximum oil temperature not being exceeded.
reduced corrosion of the HP turbine blades.

questions.questionText:
Regarding a jet engine:
1) The maximum thrust decreases as the pressure altitude increases.
2) The specific fuel consumption is inversely proportional to pressure altitude, at constant TAS.
The take-off of a jet aeroplane may be performed with "reduced thrust" in order to:
options: reduce the risk of engine failure during take-off.

questions.questionText:
Regarding a jet engine:
1) The maximum thrust increases as the pressure altitude decreases.
2) The specific fuel consumption increases slightly as the pressure altitude decreases at constant TAS.
The engine pressure ratio (EPR) is the ratio of:
options: the total turbine outlet pressure to the total compressor inlet pressure.
total compressor outlet pressure to the total turbine outlet pressure.
total compressor inlet pressure to the total turbine outlet pressure.
the total turbine inlet pressure to the total compressor inlet pressure.

questions.questionText:
Regarding a jet engine:
1) The maximum thrust decreases as the pressure altitude increases.
2) The specific fuel consumption decreases slightly as the pressure altitude increases at constant TAS.
The selection of bleed air to "ON" will cause:
options: an increase in exhaust temperature, gas generator speed and power.
a decrease in power and an increase in exhaust temperature.
a reduction of torque and gas generator RPM with an increase of exhaust temperature.
a decrease in gas generator RPM with no change in power or exhaust temperatures.

questions.questionText:
Regarding a jet engine:
1) The maximum thrust remains constant as the pressure altitude increases.
2) The specific fuel consumption remains constant as the pressure altitude increases at constant TAS.

The most favourable conditions to apply the flexible take-off procedure are:
1) high take-off mass.
2) low take-off mass.
3) high outside temperature.
4) low outside temperature.
5) high atmospheric pressure.
6) low atmospheric pressure.
The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
Using compressor bleed air to power systems:
options: is limited to the phases of take-off and landing.
has no influence on aircraft performance.

questions.questionText: The primary purpose of a turbocharger is to:
options: provide a richer mixture at high altitudes.
provide a leaner mixture at sea level.

questions.questionText: During a climb in a standard atmosphere with constant MAP and RPM indications and at a constant mixture setting, the power output of a piston engine:
options: only stays constant if the speed control lever is pushed forward.

questions.questionText: Assume an initial condition at a high cruise altitude with a constant speed propeller. What will happen if the altitude is decreased while the throttle remains fully open and the waste gate is seized in the cruise position?
options: The blade angle may reach the full fine limit.
The power of the engine will decrease.
The manifold absolute pressure (MAP) value will stay constant.
The manifold absolute pressure (MAP) value may exceed the maximum allowed value.

questions.questionText: The power of a normally aspirated piston engine decreases during a climb with a constant power lever setting because of the decreasing:
A turbocharger system in a reciprocating engine is normally driven by:
options: an electrically activated hydraulically powered clutch.

questions.questionText: A twin-spool engine with a bypass mass flow of 250 kg/s and a HP compressor mass flow of 250 kg/s has a bypass ratio of:
When the combustion gases pass through a turbine the:
In a gas turbine engine, the maximum gas temperature is attained:
options: in the cooling airflow around the flame tube.
at the entry to the exhaust unit.

questions.questionText: In a multi-spool turbofan engine, the fan is driven by:
options: all three turbines since they are on a common shaft with the compressor.

questions.questionText: The by-pass air in a turbo fan engine has the effect of a lower specific fuel consumption by causing an overall:
options: increase in average exhaust gas flow velocity thus increasing engine pressure ratio.
decrease in combustion chamber temperature and higher thermal efficiency.
increase in the mean jet temperature and increase in thermal efficiency.
decrease in average exhaust gas flow velocity and a higher mass flow.

questions.questionText: Refer to figure 021-19. The engine type represented in the annex is:
The fan stage of a turbo-fan engine is driven by:
options: the High Pressure (HP) compressor through reduction gearing.
airflow drawn across it by the HP compressor.

questions.questionText: In a free turbine engine, the energy delivered by the free turbine stages:
options: is used to drive the compressor and the main rotor gearbox.
is dependent on the rotational speed of the gas generator.
is used to drive the compressor only.
is not connected with the rotation speed of the generator.

questions.questionText:
Given the following information about an aeroplane with a turbojet engine:
• mass air flow 50 kg/s
• TAS 90 m/s
• exhaust nozzle gas velocity 150 m/s
• exhaust nozzle static pressure 1 050 hPa
• ambient static pressure 1 000 hPa
• cross-sectional area of the nozzle 0.10 m²
Net engine thrust equals:
The effect of ram air on the operation of a gas turbine engine is:
options: an increase in the indicated airspeed (IAS) at which the aircraft should be flown for optimum range.
a lower efficiency of the engine with an increase in aircraft speed.
an increase in the engine rpm for take-off.
an increase in the efficiency of the engine.

questions.questionText: In a single spool gas turbine engine, the compressor RPM is:

The bypass ratio:
1) is the ratio of bypass air mass flow to HP compressor mass flow.
2) can be determined from the inlet air volume flow and the HP compressor volume flow.
In the airflow through a single-spool axial flow turbo-jet engine, the axial velocity of the air is greatest:
options: on exit from the propelling nozzle.

questions.questionText: In a gas turbine engine, the propelling jet is generated by:
options: expansion of hot gases resulting in a conversion of potential energy (pressure) into kinetic energy (velocity).
compression of hot gases resulting in a conversion of potential energy (pressure) into kinetic energy (velocity).
compression of hot gases resulting in a conversion of kinetic energy (velocity) into (mechanical) work.
expansion of hot gases resulting in a conversion of potential energy (pressure) into (mechanical) work.

questions.questionText: For a subsonic airflow, in a divergent duct the:
options: static pressure decreases, speed increases and static temperature increases.
static pressure decreases, speed increases and static temperature decreases.
static pressure increases, speed decreases and static temperature increases.
static pressure increases, speed decreases and static temperature decreases.

questions.questionText: Specific fuel consumption for a turbo-prop engine can be expressed in kg per:
options: hour per unit of shaft power.

questions.questionText: Refer to figure 021-15. The gas turbine illustrated is of the following type:
options: single shaft turbine and axial compressor.
single shaft turbine and centrifugal compressor.

questions.questionText: The principle of aeroplane propulsion is to generate a propelling force by:
options: pressurising air or gas in order to obtain a reaction force.
heating up air in order to obtain a reaction force.
accelerating air or gas in order to obtain a reaction force.
generating a high velocity jet pushing against the outside air.

questions.questionText: A twin-spool engine with an inlet air mass flow of 500 kg/s and a bypass mass flow of 250 kg/s has a bypass ratio (BPR) of:
Jet engine total efficiency indicates the efficiency at which:
options: chemical power in the fuel is transformed into propulsive power (T x V).
the increase of jet kinetic energy is transformed into propulsive energy.
chemical power in the fuel is transformed into an increase of jet kinetic energy per second.
potential energy in the jet is transformed into propulsive energy.

questions.questionText: During take-off, the fan in a high by-pass ratio turbofan engine produces:
options: the greater part of the thrust.
the lesser part of the thrust.

questions.questionText: For a subsonic airflow, air passing through a divergent duct:
options: decreases in velocity and increases in pressure.
increases in velocity and decreases in pressure.

questions.questionText: Refer to figure 021-17. You can find the inlet duct between the stations:
For a fan jet engine, the by-pass ratio is the:
options: hot air mass flow divided by the fuel flow.
hot air mass flow divided by the cold air mass flow.
fuel flow divided by hot air mass flow.
cold air mass flow divided by the hot air mass flow.

questions.questionText:
Both gas turbine and piston engines use a cycle made up of induction, compression, combustion and exhaust phases. Which of these statements about a gas turbine are correct or incorrect?
1) The process in a gas turbine engine is continuous.
2) Theoretically the combustion occurs at constant pressure.
A turbofan engine with an inlet air mass flow of 1 000 kg/s and a HP compressor mass flow of 111 kg/s has a bypass ratio of:

Given:
p0 = static ambient pressure
t0 = static ambient temperature
m = air mass flow
Vv = True Air Speed
Vj = jet efflux (exhaust) velocity
pj = jet efflux (exhaust) static pressure
tj = jet efflux (exhaust) static temperature
A = exhaust cross-sectional area
The thrust T of a jet engine is:
Refer to figure 021-17. You can find the low pressure (LP) compressor between the stations:

The bypass ratio:
1) is the ratio of bypass air mass flow to HP compressor mass flow.
2) can be determined from the inlet air mass flow and the HP compressor mass flow.
In a free turbine engine:
options: the compressor and power output shaft are mechanically connected.
there is no mechanical connection between the compressor and the power output shaft.
its power output shaft may be connected to either a compressor or another turbine.
the air enters the compressor via the turbine inlet.

questions.questionText: Assuming the jet pipe is not choked, the equation to calculate jet engine thrust T (with mass flow m, airspeed V_V and jet velocity V_J) is:
A turbofan engine with an inlet air mass flow of 300 kg/s and a bypass mass flow of 250 kg/s has a bypass ratio of:

Both gas turbine and piston engines use a cycle made up of induction, compression, combustion and exhaust phases. Which of these statements about a gas turbine are correct or incorrect?
1) The process in a gas turbine engine is continuous.
2) Theoretically the combustion occurs at constant volume.
In a theoretical gas turbine cycle, combustion takes place at constant:

Refer to figure 021-19
The station numbers before and after the HP compressor are:

Refer to figure 021-17
The station numbers before and after the HP compressor are:

The bypass ratio:
1) is the ratio of inlet air mass flow to HP compressor mass flow.
2) can be determined from the inlet air volume flow and the HP compressor volume flow.

Refer to figure 021-17
The engine type represented in the drawing is a:
The thermal efficiency of a gas turbine engine will increase with a:
A twin-spool gas turbine engine is one in which the:
options: LP turbine drives both the LP compressor and the HP compressor.
LP compressor drives the HP compressor.
turbines are free to rotate at speeds that differ from their associated compressors.
LP turbine drives the LP compressor and the HP turbine drives the HP compressor.

questions.questionText:
Refer to figure 021-19
You can find the low pressure (LP) compressor between the stations:

The bypass ratio:
1) is the ratio of the volume of air passing through the inlet to that through the HP compressor.
2) can be determined from the inlet air mass flow and the bypass mass flow.
A twin-spool engine with an inlet air mass flow of 220 kg/s and a HP compressor mass flow of 220 kg/s has a bypass ratio of:

Refer to figure 021-17
What is located between station 4 and 5?
In a subsonic convergent duct the:
options: pressure increases, velocity decreases and temperature increases.
pressure increases, velocity decreases and temperature decreases.
temperature increases, velocity increases and pressure decreases.
velocity increases, pressure decreases and temperature decreases.

questions.questionText: A turbofan engine with a bypass mass flow of 888 kg/s and a HP compressor mass flow of 111 kg/s has a bypass ratio of:

Both gas turbine and piston engines use a cycle made up of induction, compression, combustion and exhaust phases. Which of these statements about a gas turbine are correct or incorrect?
1) The process in a gas turbine engine is intermittent.
2) Theoretically the combustion occurs at constant pressure.
Both gas turbine and piston engines utilise a cycle of induction, compression, combustion and exhaust. However in the gas turbine these processes are ___ and combustion occurs at ___
Name the correct function of the main components of a turbine engine system.
options: A starter and igniter ignite the air-fuel mixture in the combustion system.
High pressure fuel pumps and a compressor increase the pressure of the air-fuel mixture for starting.
A turbine compresses and ignites the air-fuel mixture in the combustion chamber.
A combustion chamber starter and bleed air ignite the air-fuel mixture.

questions.questionText:
The static thrust of a turbo-jet:
1) equals the product of the exhaust gas mass flow and the exhaust gas velocity.
2) is obtained by pressure of the exhaust gas on the ambient air.
3) is equivalent to zero mechanical power since the aeroplane is not moving.
4) is independent of the outside air temperature.
The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:

Refer to figure 021-19
The station numbers before and after the LP turbine are:
One of the advantages of multiple-spool jet engine designs is that:
options: a compressor stall cannot occur under any condition.
if one spool seizes, the remaining spool(s) will continue to operate normally.
a smaller air starter driving only a single spool can be used.

questions.questionText: By-pass ratio in a turbine engine is the ratio of the:
options: speed of the combusted air to the speed of the by-pass air.
tertiary air mass flow to the primary air mass flow.
intake air pressure to the turbine delivery air pressure.
cold air mass flow to the hot air mass flow.

questions.questionText: When the combustion gases pass through a turbine stage the:
About a twin spool compressor.
options: When operating at very high RPMs, the later stages within the high pressure spool will have a large negative angle of attack.
The low pressure spool runs at a lower RPM than the high pressure spool.
Both spools run at the same RPM.
The low pressure spool runs at a very high RPM, thus preventing the onset of compressor stall.

questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake?
1) The speed increases in flow direction.
2) The total pressure increases in flow direction.
One of the advantages of an annular combustion chamber is:
options: a reduced thermal stress on the turbine.

questions.questionText:
Which of these statements about a reaction turbine are correct or incorrect?
1) The pressure drops across the nozzle guide vanes.
2) The pressure rises across the rotor blades.

Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake?
1) The static pressure increases in flow direction.
2) The total temperature remains constant in flow direction.

The characteristics of an axial compressor are:
1) a low pressure ratio by stage.
2) a high pressure ratio by stage.
3) the possibility of compressing a large mass airflow.
4) the inability of compressing a large mass airflow.
The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
The blades in an axial flow compressor decrease in size from the:
options: high pressure to the low pressure stages in order to allow for expansion at increased velocity.
low pressure to the high pressure stages in an attempt to sustain axial velocity.
root to the tip in order to maintain a constant angle of attack.
root to the tip in order to increase tip clearance.

questions.questionText: When a gas turbine engine is fitted with can-type combustion chambers:
options: a total of only two igniters are usually fitted since the chambers are inter-connected.
each chamber has its own igniter.
each chamber is fitted with two igniters.
each chamber is fitted with one igniter and one glow plug.

questions.questionText: When a gas turbine engine accelerates from idle to take-off thrust and the compressor RPM is too high in comparison with the air inlet velocity, this may result in:
options: supersonic airflow at the compressor blade trailing edges.
a compressor blade airflow angle of attack that is too small.

questions.questionText: In addition to providing 'customer' bleed for aeroplane systems, air may be bled from compressors of a turbofan engine to increase stall margin:
options: and recover from stall at high rotor speed.
and prevent stall at high rotor speed.

questions.questionText: Static gas temperature decrease when flowing at a subsonic speed through a jet engine exhaust nozzle because the:
options: velocity of the gas stream decreases.
static pressure increases when passing the nozzle.
ambient air cools the exhaust casing.

questions.questionText:
Which of these statements about compressor stall are correct or incorrect?
1) A compressor stall is most likely to occur in a compressor designed for a high pressure ratio operating at low RPM.
2) A compressor stall is most likely to occur when the air flow stagnates in the rear stages of the compressor.
Compare the pressure ratio of a centrifugal compressor with the pressure ratio of an axial flow compressor. A typical value of the pressure ratio of an axial compressor is:
The primary reason for a limitation being imposed on the temperature of gas flow in a gas turbine is to:
options: prevent damage to the jet pipe from overheating.
ensure that the maximum acceptable temperature within the combustion chamber is not exceeded.
ensure that the maximum acceptable temperature at the turbine blades is not exceeded.
prevent overheating and subsequent creep of the nozzle guide vanes.

questions.questionText: A booster or LP compressor in a twin-spool turbofan:
options: rotates at the same rotor speed as the HP turbine.
rotates at a rotor speed different from both fan, HP compressor and HP turbine.
rotates at the same rotor speed as the HP compressor.
rotates at the same rotor speed as the fan.

questions.questionText: Compare the pressure ratio of a centrifugal compressor stage with the pressure ratio of an axial flow compressor stage. A typical value of the pressure ratio of an axial compressor stage is:
Which of the following statements regarding variable inlet guide vanes (VIGVs) and variable stator vanes (VSVs) on a high-bypass engines is correct?
options: VIGVs are located in the front of the fan and control both fan and compressor air flow. VSVs are used in the compressor only and are used to increase pressure ratio.
If VIGVs move towards the open position, VSVs move towards the closed position.
VIGVs are located at the front of the HP compressor and, together with the VSVs, control compressor airflow.
VIGVs prevent LP compressor stall and VSVs prevent HP compressor stall.

questions.questionText: Each stage of an axial compressor comprises:
options: a row of inlet guide vanes followed by a row of rotor blades.
a row of rotor blades followed by a row of inlet guide vanes.
inlet guide vanes followed by a row of rotor blades and a row of stator vanes.
a row of rotor blades followed by a row of stator vanes.

questions.questionText: The internal geometry of a turbofan intake for a subsonic commercial aeroplane is:
options: divergent in order to reduce airflow velocity and increase static pressure in front of the fan.
divergent in order to increase both airflow velocity and static pressure in front of the fan.
convergent in order to increase airflow velocity and reduce static pressure in front of the fan.
convergent in order to reduce airflow velocity in front of the fan.

questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The static pressure decreases in flow direction. 2) The total temperature increases in flow direction.
During the ram recovery process in a gas turbine engine subsonic intake, the:
The primary function of the diffuser, located between the compressor and the combustion chamber, of a gas turbine engine is to:
options: increase the flow velocity of the compressed air.
convert kinetic energy into mechanical energy.

questions.questionText: Turbine blade stages may be classed as either "impulse" or "reaction". In an impulse blade section the pressure ___ across the nozzle guide vanes and ___ across the rotor blades.
In a gas turbine engine the "creep" phenomenon is the most severe in the:
One of the advantages of annular combustion chamber over a multiple can is that:
options: the thermal load distribution on the HP turbine is more favourable.
engine length is shorter because no diffuser is required.

questions.questionText: A gas turbine engine turbine section may employ active clearance control:
options: in order to reduce "creep" in the blades.
as a method of attaching the stators and rotors to the hub of the disc.
to provide enhanced clearances between the blade tips and the casing.
in order to cool the disc.

questions.questionText: Compressor surge may be controlled by means of:
In a compressor stage, if the axial air velocity increases more than the rotor blade velocity the:
options: pressure ratio will be too high.

questions.questionText: Compressor stall is due to:
options: an excessively low angle of attack of the rotor blades.
an excessively high angle of attack of the rotor blades.

questions.questionText: A stage in an axial compressor:
options: is made of ring of stator blades followed by a rotor disc.
is made of a rotor disc followed by a ring of stator blades.
has a compression ratio in the order of 2.1.
has a compression ratio in the order of 0.8.

questions.questionText: An axial compressor, when operating below its design speed, has:
options: a tendency to stall in the rear stages.
a tendency to stall in the front stages.
a tendency to stall in the centre stages.

questions.questionText: The compressor blades in a rotating gas turbine engine are held in position by:
options: the mountings and the resultant of aerodynamic and centrifugal forces.
the mountings and thermal expansion only.

questions.questionText: Compressor surge during gas turbine engine acceleration is prevented by:
options: variable setting type nozzle guide vanes.
controlling the fuel flow (by the FCU - Fuel Control Unit).

questions.questionText: Turbine blade stages may be classed as either IMPULSE or REACTION. In an impulse turbine stage:
options: the pressure drops across the stator blades and remains constant across the rotor blades.
the pressure remains constant across the stator blades and rises constant across the rotor blades.
the pressure remains constant across the stator blades and drops across the rotor blades.
the pressure rises across the stator blades and remains constant across the rotor blades.

questions.questionText: The turbine in a gas turbine engine generates shaft power using:
options: expansion of hot gas followed by conversion of kinetic energy (velocity) into (mechanical) work.
compression of hot gas followed by conversion of potential energy (pressure) into kinetic energy (velocity).
expansion of hot gas followed by conversion of potential energy (pressure) into (mechanical) work.
compression of hot gas followed by conversion of kinetic energy into (mechanical) work.

questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The speed decreases in flow direction. 2) The total pressure remains constant in flow direction.
A diffuser in a gas turbine engine is a device in which the:
options: pressure rises at a constant velocity.

questions.questionText: The most unfavourable conditions for adequate inlet air flow of a turbofan engine are:
options: maximum thrust during climb out with strong crosswind.
idle thrust, zero forward speed with no wind.
maximum thrust at rotation speed on the ground during take-off.
maximum take-off thrust, zero forward speed and strong crosswind conditions.

questions.questionText: In the stator of a turbine, the gas velocity "V" and static pressure "p" vary as follows:
The axial compressor of a gas turbine engine usually has more stages than its driving turbine because:
options: bleed air is taken from the compressor.
the compressor consumes much more power than the turbine.
turbine speed is much higher than compressor speed.
the power output of a turbine stage is higher than the power consumption of a compressor stage.

questions.questionText: The term used to describe a permanent and cumulative deformation of the turbine blades in a gas turbine engine is:
The purpose of a compressor bleed valve is to prevent surging:
In a jet engine the propelling nozzle:
options: must be designed to obtain the correct balance of pressure and velocity to prevent flow separation.
decreases the velocity and increases the pressure in the jet pipe to provide greater thrust.
directs the hot gas stream onto the turbine at the optimum angle of attack.

questions.questionText: Refer to figure 021-16. A turbine stage is represented by:
Which of these statements about a reaction turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure drops across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure drops across the rotor blades.
One of the purposes of the bleed valves fitted to axial flow compressors is to:
options: reduce the tendency to compressor stall.
control the acceleration time of the engine.
spill compressor air should the engine overspeed thus controlling the speed.
enable an external air supply to spin up the compressor for engine starting.

questions.questionText: The purpose of variable by-pass valves in a high bypass turbofan engine between the LP compressor and the HP compressor is to:
options: prevent fan surge at high rotor speeds (high rpm).
prevent HPC stall during engine start.
prevent fan stall and LP compressor stall during engine start and low rotor speeds.
provide sufficient air to the HPC during engine start.

questions.questionText: Which of these statements about an impulse turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure rises across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure rises across the rotor blades.
Increasing the frequency of variations in thrust setting during normal operation is detrimental to the life of HP turbine blades because:
options: fatigue due to vibration is more severe.
low cycle fatigue damage is more severe.
the EGT limit would be exceeded more often.

questions.questionText: One advantage of having three instead of two spools in some high-bypass turbofan engine is that:
options: fewer variable stator vanes and variable inlet guide vanes are required to prevent compressor stall at low rotor speeds.
fewer variable stator vanes and variable inlet guide vanes are required to prevent compressor stall at high rotor speeds.
there is a lower fuel consumption and more thrust due to higher overall pressure ratio.
there is a lower fuel consumption due to a higher overall pressure ratio.

questions.questionText: Which of these statements about a reaction turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure drops across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure remains constant across the rotor blades.
Which of these statements about a reaction turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure rises across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure drops across the rotor blades.

The characteristics of a centrifugal compressor are: 1) a high pressure ratio by stage. 2) a large diameter. 3) a low pressure ratio by stage. 4) a small diameter. The combination containing all of the correct statements is:
Some gas turbine engines have multiple stages in the HP and LP turbines:
options: to help stabilise the pressure between the compressor and the turbine.
to reduce noise in the propelling nozzle.
because one stage does not generate sufficient shaft power.
to facilitate balancing of the turbine assembly.

questions.questionText: The function of the drain valve in a gas turbine combustion chamber is to:
options: remove unburned fuel from the combustion chamber.
vent the combustion chamber after engine shutdown.
check whether the fuel nozzles leak fuel after engine shutdown.

questions.questionText: In which part of a gas turbine engine are high temperature requirements most critical for the materials used?
options: The combustion chamber and exhaust nozzle.
The last part of the HP compressor and front part of the HP turbine.
The combustion chamber and front part of the HP turbine.

questions.questionText: The purpose of the blow-in-doors at the air inlets is to:
options: serve to increase the relative velocity at the first compressor stage.
provide the engine with additional air at high power settings and low air speeds.
provide the engine with additional air at high power settings at cruising speed.
feed cooling air to the engine cowling.

questions.questionText: Which of these statements about an impulse turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure drops across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure remains constant across the rotor blades.

Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The dynamic pressure decreases in flow direction. 2) The static temperature increases in flow direction.
Creep in a gas turbine engine is:
options: the elastic increase in length of metal parts due to tensile stress and high temperatures.
expansion of metal parts due to temperature increase.
the permanent increase in length of metal parts due to a combination of tensile stress and high temperatures over a prolonged period.
crack formation in metal parts due to cyclic mechanical loads and high temperatures.

questions.questionText: In the axial flow compressor the flow duct is tapered. Its shape is calculated so as to:
options: maintain a constant axial speed in cruising flight.
reduce the axial speed, whatever the engine rating.
reduce the axial speed in cruising flight.
maintain a constant axial speed whatever the engine rating.

questions.questionText: Which of these statements about compressor stall are correct or incorrect? 1) A compressor stall is most likely to occur in a compressor designed for a high pressure ratio operating at high RPM. 2) A compressor stall is most likely to occur when the air flow stagnates in the rear stages of the compressor.

Refer to figure 021-16
An axial compressor stage is represented by:

Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The dynamic pressure decreases in flow direction. 2) The static temperature decreases in flow direction.

Which of these statements about an impulse turbine are correct or incorrect?
1) The pressure remains constant across the nozzle guide vanes.
2) The pressure drops across the rotor blades.
One of the functions of a gas turbine engine subsonic intake is to:
Which of the following parameters remains constant in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake?
Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake?
In a turbo-jet engine, the purpose of the turbine is to:
options: clear the burnt gases, the expansion of which provide the thrust.
drive the compressor by using part of the energy from the exhaust gases.
compress the air in order to provide a better charge of the combustion chamber.
drive devices like pumps, regulator, generator.

questions.questionText: Compressor stall in a gas turbine engine:
options: is unlikely to occur in a compressor with many stages.
does not occur with well-designed compressor blades.
can be caused by an axial airflow velocity that is low relative to the rotor speed.

questions.questionText:
Which of these statements about compressor stall are correct or incorrect?
1) A compressor stall is most likely to occur in a compressor designed for a high pressure ratio operating at high RPM.
2) A compressor stall is most likely to occur when the air flow stagnates in the front stages of the compressor.
In a compressor stage of a gas turbine engine, the sequence is:
Engine temperature limitations of a gas turbine engine are usually imposed due to temperature limitations on the:
After air has passed through the compressor of a gas turbine engine the:
options: velocity will be higher than the inlet velocity.
velocity will be the same as the inlet velocity.
pressure will be the same as the inlet pressure.
temperature will be higher than the inlet temperature.

questions.questionText: In a gas turbine combustion chamber:
options: the gas temperature is highest at the chamber outlet.
secondary air is required for cooling of the inner casing.
the axial velocity of the gas stream is greatest behind the swirl vanes.
the gas pressure increases in an axial (downstream) direction.

questions.questionText:
Which of these statements about a reaction turbine are correct or incorrect?
1) The pressure remains constant across the nozzle guide vanes.
2) The pressure rises across the rotor blades.

The disadvantages of axial flow compressors compared to centrifugal flow compressors are:
1) more expensive to manufacture.
2) lower airflow.
3) greater vulnerability to foreign object damage.
4) lower pressure ratio.

The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
One of the purposes of compressor bleed valves is to:
options: decrease the load on the compressor when the engine is approaching maximum RPM.
increase the airflow across the rear compressor stages, whilst that across the early stages is decreased.
decrease the airflow across the rear compressor stages, whilst that across the early stages is increased.
limit the acceleration period of the engine.

questions.questionText: The air flow passages between adjacent axial compressor rotor blades are divergent, but the absolute exit velocity is higher than the absolute entry velocity of the rotor. This is because:
options: the gas expands between the blades.
the compressor rotational velocity slows down the air flow.
it is a constant temperature process.
part of the compressor work delivered to the air is converted into kinetic energy.

questions.questionText: The rotor blades of an axial compressor of a gas turbine engine are often mounted on a disk or drum using a "loose fit" because this:
options: limits damage to the blades due to vibration.

questions.questionText: Ice formation on turbofan engine intakes is usually:
options: prevented by using compressor bleed air.
prevented by using turbine bleed air.

questions.questionText: The purpose of a variable bypass valve, fitted between the LP and HP compressor of an axial flow compressor, is to:
options: prevent the engine from an overspeed.
enable to start the engine with an external air supply.
control the acceleration of the engine.
prevent stall in the LP compressor.

questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake?
1) The dynamic pressure remains constant in flow direction.
2) The static temperature remains constant in flow direction.
Severe compressor stall can be indicated amongst others by:
options: an increase in engine pressure ratio (EPR) and a decrease in TGT and fuel flow.
a rise in TGT and an increase in fuel flow.
a decrease in TGT and a change in vibration level.
a rise in turbine gas temperature (TGT) and a change in vibration level.

questions.questionText: In a gas turbine engine, compressor blades, which are not rigidly fixed in position when the engine is stationary, take up a rigid position when the engine is running due to:
options: the resultant of aerodynamic and centrifugal forces.

questions.questionText: Creep of turbine blades is caused by:
options: bending stresses set up by gas pressure.
high blade temperature whilst under centrifugal loading.

questions.questionText: One reason to fit a tip shroud to a turbine blade is to:
options: provide a seal for the cooling airflow.

questions.questionText:
The function of the swirl vanes round the fuel nozzles is to:
1) increase air pressure at the fuel nozzles.
2) reduce the average axial flow speed in order to stabilise the flame front.
3) generate a swirl of incoming air to enhance mixing of the fuel with air.

The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:

Which of these statements about an impulse turbine are correct or incorrect?
1) The pressure rises across the nozzle guide vanes.
2) The pressure drops across the rotor blades.
The compressor pressure ratio of an axial flow compressor is primarily determined by the:
On a FADEC gas turbine engine during normal flight, the power for the FADEC's sensors comes from:
options: the FADEC power source(s) on the engine.

questions.questionText: Concerning the starting procedure of a gas turbine engine:
options: If the HP fuel valve is opened at a relatively high compressor rpm, this may result in a hot start.
If the air starter seizes, the engine can still be started by means of another air bleed source.
the selected igniters are activated before fuel is delivered to the fuel nozzles.
the higher the starter air pressure, the higher the EGT during starting.

questions.questionText: The FADEC of a gas turbine engine normally includes:
options: an engine overspeed and/or an EGT protection function.
an overheat protection for the electronic engine control unit.
an excessive fuel flow detection function.

questions.questionText: A magnetic plug in an engine oil system can be used to:
options: collect carbon found in the oil.
prevent metallic particles from entering the oil system.

questions.questionText: In a gas turbine engine, the power changes are normally made by controlling the amount of:
options: air entering the compressor and fuel entering the combustion chambers.
air leaving the compressor by opening or closing of bleed valves.

questions.questionText: The purpose of a chip detector in the oil system of an engine/gearbox is to indicate that:
options: the oil temperature is too high.
there are metal particles in the oil.

questions.questionText: The thrust reversers of jet engines may use clamshell doors in order to:
options: stop the flow of the exhaust gas stream.
change the direction of the exhaust gas stream.
absorb any surge in engine thrust.
reduce the pressure of the exhaust gas stream by diffusing it to atmosphere.

questions.questionText:
A reverse thrust alert on the flight deck occurs when:
1) the reverser doors are unlocked with the reverse lever in the stowed position.
2) the reverser doors are locked with the reverse lever in the stowed position.
3) the reverser doors remain stowed with the reverse lever in the deployed position.
4) the reverser doors have moved to the reverse thrust position with the reverse lever in the deployed position.

The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
Engine auxiliary gearboxes:
options: provide the drives for a wide variety of services and may even provide for the support of the engine in the airframe.
never provide a torque sensing facility.
are driven by the output from the free power turbine.
always provide drives for fuel pumps, oil pumps, speed sensing and torque sensing.

questions.questionText: The pressurising function of the pressurising and dump valve in a gas turbine engine fuel system is:
options: to admit fuel to the nozzles only if the fuel pressure is high enough for sufficient atomisation.
to prevent fuel leakage in the combustion chamber after engine shutdown.
to prevent overpressure at the fuel nozzles.
to admit oil to the fuel-oil heat exchanger preventing ice crystals blocking the fuel lines.

questions.questionText:
The FADEC (Full Authority Digital Engine Control) provides:
1) engine automatic shut-down if maximum N1 is exceeded.
2) engine automatic shut-down if maximum EGT is exceeded.
3) automatic thrust rating control.
4) fully automatic engine starting.

The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
The fuel-oil heat exchanger:
options: cools the oil and heats the fuel.
cools both the oil and the fuel.

questions.questionText: After a successful start of a high bypass turbofan engine, the air starter:
options: is disengaged by the coupling between engine N1 spool and starter by the use of centrifugal forces.
is disengaged by using a clutch that is controlled by the main engine control.
remains engaged and rotates continuously with the N2 spool.
is disengaged by the coupling between engine N2 spool and starter by the use of centrifugal forces.

questions.questionText: An aircraft flying in conditions of continuous heavy rain and high ambient temperatures may require the precautionary use of:
options: both engine intake anti-icing and airframe de-icing.

questions.questionText: Engine oil reservoirs are sometimes located at the front of the engine:
options: to allow the hot oil to heat the air intake.
to utilise the structure necessary to contain the oil to form the engine accessory gearbox and provide mounting points for oil and fuel pumps.
because it is the most distant position from the exhaust.
to provide an attachment point for the mounting of the compressor and turbine bearings.

questions.questionText: The reason for having a low pressure fuel-cooled oil cooler in a recirculatory type oil system is to:
options: cool the oil and heat the fuel.
cool both the oil and the fuel.

questions.questionText: Fuel for gas turbine engines is usually heated in order to:
options: prevent icing-up of the LP filter.

questions.questionText:
The use of igniters in a gas turbine engine is required:
1) throughout the operating range of the engine
2) during engine accelerations
3) for ground starts
4) for in-flight relights
5) during turbulence in flight
6) under heavy precipitation or in icing conditions

The combination which regroups all the correct statements is:
For a turbine engine, the self-sustaining speed relates to the minimum RPM at which the engine:
options: operates most efficiently in the cruise.
is designed to IDLE after starting.
will continue to operate without any starter motor assistance.
will enable the generators to supply bus-bar voltage.

questions.questionText: The primary reason for venting an engine oil tank is to:
options: prevent excessive pressure in the tank.
allow for expansion of hot oil.

questions.questionText: The meaning of abbreviation "FADEC" on a gas turbine engine is:
Concerning the fuel system high-pressure shut-off valve (HPSOV) of a gas turbine engine:
options: manual HPSOV control from the cockpit is not possible with FADEC equipped engines.
ignition is activated before fuel is delivered by the HPSOV to the fuel nozzles.
the HPSOV is mounted in the wing front spar near the pylon.
the HPSOV is located between the HP fuel pump and the fuel control unit.

questions.questionText: The primary input signals for a hydro-mechanical gas turbine engine fuel control system include:
options: N1, N2, bypass ratio, fuel shut-off, thrust lever angle.
N2, turbine inlet temperature, fuel shut-off, generator, thrust lever angle.
N2, bypass ratio, EPR, fuel shut-off, thrust lever angle.
N2, compressor discharge pressure, compressor inlet temperature, fuel shut-off, thrust lever angle.

questions.questionText: In an ignition system during a normal start of a gas turbine engine, igniter activation:
options: should commence prior to starter motor engagement to prevent a hot start.
should commence prior to fuel entering the combustion chamber.
commences upon starter motor engagement, and is deactivated at a rotor speed of approximately 15% below idle rpm.
should commence after fuel has entered the combustion chamber.

questions.questionText: The oil system of a gas turbine engine may be fitted with magnetic plugs, or chip detectors, that:
options: provide warning of impending failure without having to remove the filters for inspection.
monitor oil pressure and oil temperature.
are fitted in the pressure line upstream of the oil filter.
dispense with the requirement to fit an oil filter.

questions.questionText: If a gas turbine engine fuel/oil heat exchanger is located downstream of the HP fuel pump, internal leakage of the heat exchanger will cause the oil level to:
options: fall and the oil jets eventually to become clogged.
rise and the fuel nozzles eventually to become clogged.

questions.questionText: The purpose of magnetic chip detectors is to:
options: perform the function of a micron filter.
remove large items of debris from the system.
increase lubricating oil adhesion to main surfaces.

questions.questionText:
The electronic engine control unit of a gas turbine engine FADEC uses data from:
1) aircraft systems.
2) cockpit display units.
3) its own engine sensors.
4) electronic engine control units of the other engines(s).
The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:

The FADEC (Full Authority Digital Engine Control):
1) provides fully automatic engine starting.
2) modifies aircraft airspeed in order to allow for the lowest fuel consumption.
3) counters any yaw movement in case of engine failure.
4) provides thrust reverser control.
The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
A FADEC on a gas turbine engine:
options: consists of two identical channels cross linked through an non engine related aircraft computer.

questions.questionText: On most high by-pass ratio (fan) engines, reverse thrust is achieved by reversing:
options: only the direction of the fan airflow.
the direction of rotation of the fan.
only the direction of the exhaust gas flow.
the direction of both the exhaust gas flow (hot stream) and the fan airflow (cold stream).

questions.questionText: The fuel/oil heat exchanger in a turbine engine uses:
options: engine oil and is connected to the high pressure area.
hydraulic fluid and is connected to the high pressure area.
hydraulic fluid and is connected to the low pressure area.
engine oil and is connected to the low pressure area.

questions.questionText:
The FADEC (Full Authority Digital Engine Control) can provide:
1) thrust reverser control.
2) engine operation within safe limits.
3) automatic engine starting sequence.
4) automatic thrust rating control.
The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
The Throttle Lever Angle (TLA) information of a FADEC controlled gas turbine engine is provided:
options: to the hydro mechanical main engine control.
to the electronic engine control unit by a direct electrical wiring connection.
to a single dedicated channel of the FADEC.

questions.questionText: The FADEC of a gas turbine engine consists of:
options: an electronic engine control unit and the engine display units in the cockpit.
an electronic engine control unit only.
all electronic engine control units installed on the aircraft.
an electronic engine control unit and its peripherals (fuel metering, actuators, electrical wiring, sensors...).

questions.questionText: Most high-bypass turbofan engines are equipped with a cold exhaust reverser (fan reverser) only in order to:
options: prevent extra fuel consumption due to operation of the hot exhaust reverser (turbine reverser).
prevent disturbance of cold exhaust reversed airflow by the hot exhaust reversed gas flow.
reduce the risk of exhaust gas re-ingestion.
save costs and mass at the expense of a reduction in available reverse thrust.

questions.questionText:
A Full Authority Digital Engine Control (FADEC) has the following functions:
1) flow regulation (fuel, decelerations and accelerations monitoring).
2) automatic starting sequence.
3) transmissions of engine data to the pilot's instruments.
4) thrust management and protection of operation limits.
5) monitoring of the thrust reversers.
The combination regrouping all the correct statements is:
The electronic engine control of a FADEC gas turbine engine checks:
options: ACTIVE CHANNEL input and output data only.

questions.questionText: If there is a single input data failure on a FADEC gas turbine engine:
options: the electronic engine control unit uses a constant data value for computation.
the engine is set at a fail-safe thrust.
the engine continues to operate normally.

questions.questionText: Labyrinth seals in the lubrication system of a gas turbine engine are designed to provide a seal that is:
options: not completely tight, allowing some movement between rotating and static parts.
not completely tight, allowing only some radial movement between rotating and static parts.
completely tight, allowing no movement between rotating and static parts.
completely tight, allowing some axial movement between rotating and static parts.

questions.questionText:
The accessory units driven by the accessory gearbox of a turbo-jet engine are the:
1) thrust reverser pneumatic motors.
2) AC generator through its constant speed drive (CSD).
3) oil pumps.
4) hydraulic pumps.
5) high pressure fuel pumps.
The combination regrouping all the correct statements is:
To achieve reverse thrust on a turbine engine with a high by-pass ratio, generally:
options: the flow of hot stream air is reversed.
the direction of rotation of the fan is reversed.

questions.questionText: The function of a vibrator in some gas turbine ignition systems is to provide:
options: a DC current to the transformer to facilitate the generation of a higher voltage to the igniters.
an AC voltage in order to facilitate transformation to a higher voltage.
DC current if the ignition system is fed by AC voltage.
a pulsating AC voltage in order to generate a more powerful spark.

questions.questionText: The oil supply to gas turbine engine bearings is maintained in the event of the main oil filter becoming clogged by:
options: differential pressure opening the bypass valve of the main oil filter.

questions.questionText: A hung (abortive) start of a gas turbine engine may be caused by:
options: failure of the fuel to ignite in the starting sequence after the engine has been accelerated to the required rpm by the starter.
a too low pneumatic starter pressure.
the starter cutting out early in the starting sequence before the engine has accelerated to the required rpm for ignition.

questions.questionText: A gas turbine engine is run at ground idle for a period of time prior to shutdown to:
options: prevent seizure of the engine bearings.
prevent vapour lock in the fuel control and/or fuel lines.
prevent seizure of the rotor blades in their seals.
allow the engine oil to cool.

questions.questionText: The following problems may occur when starting a twin-spool turbofan engine:
options: no N2 rotation, although N1 may accelerate normally.
no N1 rotation, although N2 may accelerate normally.
a hung (abortive) start with no light-up indication after 'fuel-on' selection.
a hung (abortive) start with fuel flow indication zero after 'fuel-on' selection.

questions.questionText: With regard to a twin spool turbofan, an abortive start (hung start) will result in:
options: N2 speed stabilising at a lower than normal RPM and a subsequent increase in EGT.
N2 speed stabilising at a higher than normal RPM and a subsequent increase in EGT.
N2 speed stabilising at a lower than normal RPM and a subsequent decrease in EGT.
N2 speed stabilising at a higher than normal RPM and a subsequent decrease in EGT.

questions.questionText: An abortive (hung) start is the term used to describe an attempt to start in which the engine:
options: commences initial rotation, but fails to receive any fuel.
lights up, but fails to accelerate sufficiently.

questions.questionText: Which of the jet engine ratings below is not a certified rating?
The purpose of gas turbine engine trending is:
options: to assess engine condition using non-destructive inspection.
to enhance maintenance planning and detect anomalies.
to obtain a timely indication when an engine requires to be shut down due to problems.
to collect metal particles from the oil system.

questions.questionText:
The control of free turbine engines on turboprops, is accomplished by:
PROPELLER control lever used to select:
1) propeller RPM
2) turbine temperature
3) turbine RPM
FUEL control lever used to select:
4) propeller RPM
5) torque
6) turbine temperature
The combination which regroups all of the correct statements is:
If the main oil filter of a gas turbine engine becomes clogged, oil to the main bearings is still assured:
options: if a higher engine RPM is selected to increase oil pressure.
if breather pressure in the main compartments is increased to ensure positive lubrication of the bearings.
because fuel upstream of the oil filter will be mixed with oil to allow easier passage through the filter.
because the oil filter bypass valve opens.

questions.questionText: The primary performance indicator (cockpit instrument) for the thrust of a turbofan engine is:

Which statement is correct for a gas turbine engine with a constant speed propeller and free power turbine, when the power setting is decreased?
1) The HP spool speed decreases.
2) The EGT decreases.

Which statement is correct for a gas turbine engine with a constant speed propeller and free power turbine, when the power setting is increased?
1) The gas generator speed remains constant.
2) The EGT decreases.
Exhaust gas temperature sensing is usually taken:
options: between the stages of the gas generator turbine.
at the outlet from the combustion chamber.
between the gas generator turbine and the free power turbine.

questions.questionText: In very cold weather, a slightly higher than normal engine oil pressure during startup:
options: is unacceptable but does not require the engine to be shut down.
is unacceptable and requires the engine to be shut down.
is acceptable, if it decreases after startup.

questions.questionText:
On landing and prior to shutdown, a gas turbine engine should be run at "ground idle" for a short period.
This allows:
options: cool oil flushing of bearings, particularly those in the turbines.
the turbine blade temperature to reduce only.
the engine to be compressor washed.

questions.questionText: Vibration sensors are used in a gas turbine engine to indicate:
options: imbalance of one or both of the spools.
that there is too much free movement of the engine strut (pylon).
that tip clearance of turbine blades has decreased below a minimum value.

questions.questionText:
Which statement is correct for a gas turbine engine with a constant speed propeller and free power turbine, when the power setting is increased?
1) The HP spool speed decreases.
2) The EGT increases.
One of the causes of high oil temperature in a gas turbine engine at constant power setting is:
options: unusually high scavenge pump oil flow.

questions.questionText: If a twin-spool gas turbine engine is controlled by a conventional N2 control system, rupture of the LP shaft may result in:
Increasing the power setting of a gas turbine engine with a constant speed propeller and free power turbine causes:
options: HP spool speed to remain constant and EGT to increase.
both HP spool speed and EGT to increase.
HP spool speed to increase and EGT to remain constant.
HP spool speed to decrease and EGT to increase.

questions.questionText: A pressure relief valve in an oil system that does not seat correctly would result in:

Which statement is correct for a gas turbine engine with a constant speed propeller and free power turbine, when the power setting is increased?
1) The gas generator speed increases.
2) The EGT increases.
When a turbo-prop aeroplane decelerates during the initial part of the landing roll, a constant speed propeller within the beta range:
The most important oil system instrument indication during gas turbine engine start is:

Which statement is correct for a gas turbine engine with a constant speed propeller and free power turbine, when the power setting is increased?
1) The gas generator speed decreases.
2) The EGT remains constant.

Which statement is correct for a gas turbine engine with a constant speed propeller and free power turbine, when the power setting is increased?
1) The HP spool speed increases.
2) The EGT remains constant.
What is described by the term alpha range when talking about a turboprop?
If an abortive start (hung start) occurs when starting a gas turbine engine, the correct action is to:
options: move the thrust lever forward to increase fuel flow.

questions.questionText: Consider the variable-pitch propeller of a turbo-prop aircraft. During deceleration:
options: when braking, the propeller supplies negative thrust and absorbs engine power.
at zero power, the propeller thrust is zero and the engine power absorbed is nil.
when feathered, the propeller produces thrust and absorbs no engine power.
with propeller windmilling, the thrust is zero and the propeller supplies engine power.

questions.questionText: If air is tapped from a gas turbine HP compressor (engine N2 controlled), the effect on the engine pressure ratio (EPR) and the exhaust gas temperature (EGT) is that:
options: EPR decreases and EGT remains constant.
EPR remains constant and EGT increases.

questions.questionText: A flat rated turbofan will be temperature limited if:
options: ambient pressure is lower than standard sea level ambient pressure.
OAT is lower than flat rated temperature.
ambient pressure is higher than standard sea level ambient pressure.
OAT is higher than flat rated temperature.

questions.questionText:
Regarding a jet engine:
1) The maximum thrust increases as the pressure altitude decreases.
2) The specific fuel consumption is proportional to pressure altitude, at constant TAS.
At constant fuel flow, if engine compressor air is bled off for engine anti-icing or a similar system, the turbine temperature:
options: may rise or fall depending on which stage of the compressor is used for the bleed and the RPM of the engine at the moment of selection.

questions.questionText: Concerning the oxygen system of a passenger airplane the following is true:
options: After activation the chemical oxygen generator can also be turned off.
Passengers must leave the toilet(s) immediately after a decompression because oxygen systems are not installed in toilets.
The chemical oxygen generator incorporates a heat shield.
In case of a failure of the chemical oxygen generators, the pilot can activate the gaseous oxygen system for passengers.

questions.questionText: The chemical oxygen generator supplies oxygen for about:
Chemical oxygen generators are used to furnish oxygen to the: 1) cockpit. 2) cabin. 3) toilets. 4) smoke hood. The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
The type of an aircraft oxygen system intended for use by passengers is mostly:

The installation and use of on-board oxygen generators is such that: 1) the smoking ban is imperative when used. 2) in case of accidental drop of the "continuous flow" passenger masks, no crew action is required. 3) no trace of grease must be found in the system assembly. 4) the system's filling adaptors must be greased with non-freezable or graphite grease. The combination which regroups all of the correct statements is:
The demand valve of a diluter-demand type oxygen regulator in normal mode, operates when the:
options: pressure in the oxygen reservoir is more than 500 psi.
diluter control is in normal position.

questions.questionText: Which of these statements about chemical oxygen generators are correct or incorrect? 1) Chemical oxygen generators are often used to supply passenger oxygen. 2) The oxygen supply of chemical oxygen generators, once activated, can be switched off.
A pilot can determine the amount of oxygen in a portable bottle by observing its:

Consider the flight deck oxygen supply system. The purpose of the oxygen regulator (as a function of demand and altitude) is to: 1) decrease oxygen pressure from 1800 psi (in the bottles) down to about 50-75 psi (low pressure system). 2) supply pure oxygen. 3) supply diluted oxygen. 4) supply oxygen at normal pressure. 5) supply oxygen at emergency/positive pressure. 6) trigger the continuous cabin altitude warning at 10000 ft cabin altitude. The combination regrouping all the correct statements is:
A passenger oxygen mask is:
options: an on-demand type mask and must be used if there is smoke in the cabin.
a continuous flow mask and must be used if there is smoke in the cabin.
an on-demand type mask and in principle should not be used if there is smoke in the cabin.
a continuous flow mask and in principle should not be used if there is smoke in the cabin.

questions.questionText: When selected to normal, the oxygen/air ratio of the mixture supplied by the cockpit oxygen system regulator:
When the oxygen system of a large aeroplane is set to the diluted mode:
options: the cockpit crew breathes a mixture of oxygen/cabin air.
the same circuit is used by the crew and the passengers.
the passenger circuit does not use the chemically generated oxygen.
the passengers are supplied with a mixture of oxygen and cabin air.

questions.questionText: The disadvantages of a chemical oxygen source compared with a gaseous one for the passenger cabin are: 1) a flow which cannot be modulated. 2) less capacity. 3) risk of fire and/or explosion. The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
When quick donning masks are in use, the pilot is:
options: not able to do any radio communication.

questions.questionText: In case of smoke in the cockpit, the crew oxygen regulator must be set to:
A smoke hood is a device covering:
options: only the nose and the mouth and with a continuous oxygen flow.
the whole head and with a continuous oxygen flow.
the whole head and with an oxygen flow only on demand.
only the nose and the mouth and with an oxygen/air mix.

questions.questionText: The advantages of a chemical oxygen source for the passenger cabin are: 1) reduced weight and volume. 2) easy storage. 3) greater autonomy. 4) less risk of explosion. 5) reversible functioning. 6) no maintenance required. The combination regrouping all the correct statements is:
The replenishment of an onboard oxygen bottle must be done:
options: with a ground power unit connected, electrical power supplied.

questions.questionText: An oxygen supply system must be kept absolutely free of oil or grease as:
options: these substances catch fire spontaneously in the presence of oxygen.
the oxygen system would be contaminated.
these substances could plug the oxygen masks filters.
these substances mixed with oxygen could catch fire in the presence of a spark.

questions.questionText: The built-in passenger oxygen system can be activated by:
options: switching the diluter-demand regulator and the passenger oxygen ON.

questions.questionText: Which of these statements about chemical oxygen generators are correct or incorrect? 1) Chemical oxygen generators are used to supply the cockpit oxygen system. 2) The oxygen supply of chemical oxygen generators, once activated, cannot be switched off.

The disadvantages of a chemical oxygen source for the passenger cabin are: 1) a flow which cannot be modulated. 2) a heavy and bulky system. 3) non-reversible functioning. 4) risks of explosion. 5) poor autonomy. The combination regrouping all the correct statements is:
Regarding the oxygen system of EASA CS-25 certified aeroplane:
options: the same source of supply is used by the crew and passengers.
the seals must be carefully greased to avoid sparks.
the passenger source of supply never uses chemically generated oxygen.
with the setting on "NORMAL" the cockpit crew breathes a mixture of oxygen and cabin air.

questions.questionText: Modern pressurized transport airplanes are equipped with:
options: only one oxygen system supplying the whole aircraft.
two independent oxygen systems, one supplying the cockpit, the other the cabin.
two oxygen systems both supplying the cockpit and the cabin.

questions.questionText: If the maximum operating pressure of the oxygen system is exceeded the:
options: passenger oxygen masks will drop down.
oxygen becomes unusable for the passengers.
oxygen is discharged overboard via a safety plug.

questions.questionText: A substance which may never be used in the vicinity or on parts of an oxygen installation is:

The advantages of a gaseous oxygen source compared with a chemical source for the passenger cabin are: 1) no risk of fire and/or explosion. 2) possibility to regulate flow. 3) more capacity. The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
In modern jet transport aeroplanes, oxygen for the flight deck oxygen system is typically stored:
options: in a gaseous state or as a chemical compound.

questions.questionText: The advantages of a chemical oxygen source compared with a gaseous one for the passenger cabin are: 1) easier maintenance. 2) greater capacity. 3) reduced risk of explosion. The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:

The advantages of a gaseous oxygen source for the passenger cabin are: 1) a greater autonomy. 2) no risk of explosion. 3) reversible functioning. 4) easy storage and maintenance. 5) possibility to regulate flow. The combination regrouping all the correct statements is:
Regarding the chemical oxygen generator, to enable the oxygen to flow, the passenger must:
options: turn the oxygen valve to open.
firmly pull the door compartment of the oxygen mask storage.
operate the relevant switch in his armrest.
firmly pull the mask towards his face.

questions.questionText: Fixed oxygen systems in pressurised aeroplanes are used to provide oxygen: 1) in the event of depressurisation. 2) in the event of any passenger indisposition. 3) during a normal flight. 4) in the event of smoke or toxic fumes in the cockpit. The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:

A public transport aircraft is cruising at FL390. It is fitted with individual oxygen masks for the passengers. In the event of cabin depressurization, the passenger oxygen masks must be automatically released before the cabin pressure altitude exceeds:

Consider a flat rated twin-spool turbofan with conventional hydromechanical fuel control. When using maximum take-off thrust at an outside air temperature OAT that is below the flat rated temperature:
options: the thrust lever is always positioned at the maximum stop for take-off.
the thrust lever is not positioned at the maximum stop.

questions.questionText: An engine pressure ratio (EPR) can be defined as the ratio of:
options: compressor outlet pressure to compressor inlet total pressure.
jet pipe total pressure to combustion chamber pressure.
jet pipe total pressure to compressor inlet total pressure.
combustion chamber pressure to compressor inlet total pressure.

questions.questionText: Flat rated jet engines are designed to provide constant maximum take-off thrust up to a certain level of:
A flat rated turbofan engine has a constant maximum rated thrust level:
options: up to a specific pressure altitude.

questions.questionText: Consider a jet engine whose control is based on the engine pressure ratio (EPR): 1) with a constant EPR, the thrust decreases when the altitude increases. 2) with a constant EPR, the thrust is independent of the Mach number. 3) at same environmental conditions, a given EPR setting maintains the thrust irrespective of engine wear due to ageing. 4) the EPR is determined by the total pressure difference between the turbine outlet and the compressor inlet. 5) on take-off, in the event of icing not detected by the crew, the indicated EPR is lower than the real EPR. The combination regrouping all the correct statements is:
Concerning the flexible take-off mode, the temperature selected in the FMS is:
options: lower than the ambient airfield temperature, in order to achieve an increased power setting.
higher than the ambient airfield temperature, in order to achieve an increased power setting.
lower than the ambient airfield temperature, in order to achieve a reduced power setting.
higher than the ambient airfield temperature, in order to achieve a reduced power setting.

questions.questionText: The flexible take-off mode: 1) can be used only if the engines are new or recently overhauled. 2) reduces engine wear. 3) can be used in situations where take-off can be executed without the need for full engine power. 4) can only be used with an autothrottle. The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:

On the ground, the Auxiliary Power Unit (APU) can provide: 1) electricity. 2) air for main engine starting. 3) hydraulic pressure. 4) air for air conditioning. The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is:
A modern auxiliary power unit (APU) is designed to provide power for ground starting of an engine. It also supplies both in the air (subject of certification limitations) and on the ground:
options: either air conditioning or electrical services, but never both at the same time.
air conditioning and thrust in the event of engine failure.
air conditioning and electrical services (on the ground) electrical and hydraulic back-up services (in the air).

questions.questionText: The Auxiliary Power Unit (APU) has its own AC generator which:
options: must have the same characteristics as the main AC generator so that it can be easily coupled with the latter.
is driven at constant speed through a Constant Speed Drive (CSD) in the same way as the main AC generator.
supplies the aircraft with three-phase 115 V, 400 Hz AC.
is excited by its Generator Control Unit (GCU) as soon as the APU starts up.

questions.questionText: What would cause an APU to automatically shut-down? 1) High oil pressure 2) Low oil pressure 3) High EGT 4) Low APU speed 5) Overspeed 6) APU fire
Regarding detection systems, ion detectors are used to detect:
Smoke detector systems are installed in the:
Photoelectric cells:
options: are smoke detectors and detect the diffusion of a beam of light which occurs when the beam is interrupted by smoke. The scattering of the light increases the conductance of the cell and its output is amplified to operate a warning circuit.
are visual smoke detectors which are only fitted as alarm verification devices.
are carbon-monoxide detectors which are found mainly in aircraft of American manufacture. These devices detect concentrations of CO and activate a warning system.

are alpha-particle detectors and consist of ionisation chambers which measure alpha radiation from radium. Alpha particles are absorbed by smoke, which reduces the ionisation current of the device, to operate an alarm.

questions.questionText: Smoke detectors fitted on transport aircraft are of the following type:
How do smoke detectors operate?
options: A labyrinth optical detector - light refracts away from the photosensitive cell.
A light attenuation optical detector - light refracts towards the photosensitive cell.
A labyrinth optical detector - the photosensitive cell is blocked from the light source by smoke.
A light attenuation optical detector - light refracts away from the photosensitive cell.

questions.questionText: An engine fire detection system of a large multi-engine transport aircraft typically includes:
